timeline.description: Match kicks off at Lusail Stadium. Argentina in possession. Scoreline 0-0.

Argentina create multiple attacking sequences between the 16th and 20th minutes. Three shots are produced including a save by Lloris. Two corners are won consecutively. Argentina win two fouls in attacking positions. The sustained pressure forces France deep into their own half.

attribution.detail: Match Report — 16' to 20' Argentina attacking phase
Live text commentary — 16' to 20' Argentina pressure
Match timeline — 16' to 20' Argentina attacking sequences

timeline.description: Penalty awarded to Argentina. Dembélé fouls Di María inside the penalty area. Referee Marciniak points to the spot. Messi to take.
Messi places the ball low to the left from the penalty spot. Lloris dives right. 1-0 Argentina.
Di María scores with a left-footed finish across goal from a Mac Allister cross following an Argentina counter-attack. 2-0 Argentina.

Argentina extend their lead to 2-0 through Di María's 36th-minute goal. Argentina create repeated attacking chances and produce sustained attacking sequences after the opening goal. France are unable to establish sustained possession in the Argentina half. Importance 75: goal-scoring sequence with repeated attacking opportunities.

attribution.detail: Live text commentary — Argentina dominant after opening goal

timeline.description: Kolo Muani replaces Giroud. France trail 2-0. Attacking substitution when trailing — importance 50.
Thuram replaces Dembélé. France trail 2-0. Attacking substitution when trailing — importance 50.
Half-time whistle. First half concludes. Scoreline 2-0 Argentina. Goals from Messi (23' pen) and Di María (36').
Fernández shown a yellow card for a tactical foul.
Second half begins. France kick off trailing 2-0.

attribution.detail: Live text commentary — 46' second half begins

timeline.description: Rabiot shown a yellow card for a tactical foul.
Coman replaces Griezmann. France trail 2-0. Griezmann is France's captain and primary playmaker. Major player removal — importance 50.
Acuña replaces Di María. Argentina lead 2-0. Di María scored the second goal. Defensive substitution protecting the lead — importance 50.

France produce sustained attacking pressure between the 66th and 70th minutes. Three shots are created including one saved by Martínez. Two corners are won. France maintain sustained possession in Argentina's half with multiple entries into the attacking third and crosses into the area.

attribution.detail: Match Report — 66' to 70' France attacking phase
Live text commentary — 66' to 70' France pressure building
Minute-by-minute report — 66' France growing into the game

timeline.description: Camavinga replaces Fofana. France trail 2-0. Attacking midfield substitution when trailing — importance 50.

France intensify their attacking pressure between the 75th and 79th minutes. Four shots are produced. Martínez makes two saves. Three corners are won consecutively. Two France shots are blocked by Argentina defenders. France maintain continuous presence in the Argentina half with repeated crosses into the penalty area.

attribution.detail: Match Report — 75' to 79' France sustained attack
Live text commentary — 75' to 79' France relentless pressure
Match timeline — 75' to 79' France attacking sequence
Minute-by-minute report — 75' France camped in Argentina half

timeline.description: Paredes replaces De Paul. Argentina lead 2-0. Defensive substitution protecting the lead — importance 50.
Penalty awarded to France. Otamendi fouls Kolo Muani inside the penalty area. Referee Marciniak points to the spot. Mbappé to take.
Mbappé converts from the penalty spot. Places the ball low to the left. Martínez dives right. 2-1.

France score twice within two minutes. Mbappé converts from the penalty spot at 80'. Mbappé scores again at 81' — a volley from a Thuram pass — drawing level at 2-2. France produce repeated attacking sequences and create multiple attacking opportunities. Scoreline shifts from 2-0 to 2-2 in 97 seconds. Importance 80: goal-scoring sequence with two goals and repeated attacking opportunities.

attribution.detail: Live text commentary — 80' to 81' France double
Match timeline — 80' to 81' France attacking phase

timeline.description: Mbappé scores with a right-footed volley from a Thuram pass inside the area. 2-2.
Two-goal Argentina lead erased. France score twice within two minutes. Mbappé converts from the penalty spot at 80'. Mbappé scores a volley at 81'. Scoreline level at 2-2.
Thuram shown a yellow card for simulation.
Full-time whistle. Regulation concludes. Scoreline level at 2-2. Match proceeds to extra time. Two 15-minute periods will be played.
Giroud shown a yellow card for dissent.
Acuña shown a yellow card for a tactical foul.
First period of extra time begins. Scoreline level at 2-2. Two 15-minute periods will be played.

attribution.detail: Live text commentary — 91' extra time begins

timeline.description: Argentina create multiple attacking opportunities in the first period of extra time between the 99th and 103rd minutes. Four shots are produced including two saved by Lloris. Two corners are won consecutively. One shot is blocked by a France defender. Argentina maintain sustained possession in the France half with repeated attacking entries.

attribution.detail: Match Report — 99' to 103' Argentina extra-time pressure
Live text commentary — 99' to 103' Argentina pressing for winner
Match timeline — 99' to 103' Argentina attacking phase
Minute-by-minute report — 99' Argentina start extra time strongly

timeline.description: Second period of extra time begins. Teams change ends. Scoreline level at 2-2.

attribution.detail: Match Report — Second period of extra time
Live text commentary — 105' second period of extra time
Minute-by-minute report — 105' extra time second half

timeline.description: Messi scores from close range with a left-footed shot. Lautaro Martínez's shot is saved by Lloris. The rebound falls to Messi inside the six-yard box. 3-2 Argentina.

Argentina regain the lead in extra time. Messi scores from close range at 108' after Lautaro Martínez's shot is saved by Lloris — the rebound falls to Messi inside the six-yard box. Argentina had created multiple attacking opportunities in the first period of extra time. Importance 80: goal-scoring sequence with repeated attacking opportunities.

attribution.detail: Live text commentary — 108' Argentina retake lead

timeline.description: Disasi replaces Hernandez. France trail 3-2. Disasi is a centre-back replacing a left-back, shifting France to a back three. Formation change — importance 55.

France push for an equaliser in the closing stages of extra time between the 113th and 117th minutes. Three shots are produced. Two corners are won. A France shot is blocked by the arm of Montiel inside the penalty area resulting in a penalty award at the 118th minute. France maintain sustained pressure with repeated crosses and entries into the attacking third.

attribution.detail: Match Report — 113' to 117' France attacking phase
Live text commentary — 113' to 117' France pushing forward
Match timeline — 113' to 117' France sustained attack
Minute-by-minute report — 113' France pressure building towards penalty

timeline.description: Paredes shown a yellow card for a tactical foul.
Montiel shown a yellow card for a tactical foul. Handball inside the area.
Penalty awarded to France. The ball strikes the arm of Montiel inside the penalty area. Referee Marciniak points to the spot. Mbappé to take.
Mbappé converts from the penalty spot. Places the ball low to the left. Martínez dives right. 3-3.

France equalise for the second time. Mbappé converts from the penalty spot at 118' after the ball strikes Montiel's arm in the area. France had produced sustained attacking pressure with multiple crosses and shots in the closing stages of extra time. Importance 80: goal-scoring sequence with sustained attacking pressure.
Extra time concludes. Scoreline level at 3-3. Match proceeds to penalty shootout.

attribution.detail: Live text commentary — 120' extra time ends

timeline.description: Dybala replaces Romero. Score level at 3-3. Match enters penalty shootout. Substitution prepares for shootout format — importance 50.
Martínez shown a yellow card for post-match misconduct.
